applicable_conditions: SNR must be linear, not dB
valid_evidence: Shannon capacity is C = B log2(1 + SNR) when SNR is linear.
SNR in dB must be converted using SNR_linear = 10^(SNR_dB/10).

applicable_conditions: C and B must describe the same link and time interval
valid_evidence: Spectral efficiency is throughput or capacity divided by bandwidth.

applicable_conditions: d in km and f in MHz for the 32.44 constant
physical_constraints: path loss is non-negative for ordinary wireless links
valid_evidence: The common FSPL form FSPL_dB = 20 log10(d_km) + 20 log10(f_MHz) + 32.44 uses km and MHz.

formula: Pr = Pt Gt Gr (lambda / (4 pi d))^2
valid_evidence: Friis transmission applies to free-space line-of-sight links under far-field conditions.

applicable_conditions: all powers must be in the same linear scale
valid_evidence: SINR divides signal power by the sum of interference and noise powers in linear scale.

applicable_conditions: for power ratios expressed in dB
valid_evidence: For power ratios, x dB corresponds to a linear ratio of 10^(x/10).

applicable_conditions: threshold and average SNR in linear scale
valid_evidence: For Rayleigh fading, outage probability is 1 - exp(-gamma_th/gamma_bar).
Coherent BPSK in AWGN has BER Q(sqrt(2 Eb/N0)) with linear Eb/N0.
The Nyquist signaling limit for an ideal bandlimited channel is two symbols per second per Hz.

formula: C = log2 det(I + rho / Nt * H H^H)
valid_evidence: A common flat-fading MIMO capacity expression is log2 det(I + rho/Nt HH^H).
